# Boundary vs. software-defined perimeter


Software-defined perimeter (SDP) solutions secure access to applications and infrastructure based on user identity.

## How Boundary implements SDP principles

Boundary is fundamentally a software-defined perimeter (SDP) solution built on [zero trust](/boundary/docs/overview/zero-trust) principles: access to services is granted based on a user's verified identity and intent, not network location or perimeter trust.
Boundary is a scalable proxy that enables identity-based access with multiple layers of security controls including [dynamic credentials](/boundary/tutorials/hcp-administration/hcp-ssh-cred-injection), [context-based access](/boundary/tutorials/access-management/oidc-idp-groups), [automated host discovery](/boundary/docs/hosts), and [access auditing](/hcp/docs/boundary/audit-logging) to name a few.
